expectations.text: Detects existing reviews from the same user before posting
expectations.evidence: Real run on PR #4: skill fetched /pulls/4/reviews and detected prior review by WilcoLouwerse (id: 4120192162, submitted 2026-04-16). Correctly identified as re-review and entered re-review mode — fetching prior comments and assessing each as addressed/still-open rather than posting fresh duplicates.

expectations.text: Does NOT repost findings already covered in prior comments
expectations.evidence: Prior comments [6] (verdict parser), [7] (conduction-architecture-rules.md), [4] (max_turns) were marked ADDRESSED based on new commits. Skill only posted new inline comments for [1] hardcoded path (still open) and [2] NC_CACHE_DIR (new finding) — not redundant re-posts of resolved issues.

expectations.text: Re-review summary correctly lists addressed vs still-open items
expectations.evidence: Skill generated re-review context with explicit lists: 3 ADDRESSED ([4], [6], [7]) and 1 STILL OPEN ([1] hardcoded path). New findings from 13 new commits: NC_CACHE_DIR race condition. This matches the re-review-context.md protocol.

expectations.text: Inline comment IDs are distinct (no duplicate API call for same file:line)
expectations.evidence: Two inline comments posted with distinct IDs: 3153942991 (cron-hydra.sh pos 230) and 3153942993 (run-quality.sh pos 105). No duplicate calls to the same file/position.

notes: Re-review detection worked on the first attempt. Prior review was from 2026-04-16, 13 new commits since then. The skill's /pulls/{PR}/reviews fetch is the critical gate — without it, all 7 prior findings would have been reposted.
